Title: Bird Watching Journal
Description:
**Every flutter. Every song. Every lifer.**
The Bird Watching Journal is the field-tested companion serious birders and curious backyard watchers have been waiting for. Whether you're chasing warblers during spring migration, tallying feeder visitors with your morning coffee, or guiding someone into their first season of birding — this book turns each sighting into a record worth keeping.

**WHAT'S INSIDE (113 pages, 6 x 9 in)**
- 100 detailed sighting log pages — one rich entry per page
- 4 trip planner spreads to organize outings before you head out
- 5-page Life List section (100 species, numbered) to track every lifer
- 8-prompt Birder Profile to record your home patch and goals
- Pro tips for identification, ear-birding, and field observation
- Field notes & reflections section for migration journals and gear logs

**EVERY SIGHTING PAGE CAPTURES**
- Common name + scientific name
- Family / group + count
- Location + GPS coordinates / park / refuge
- Habitat: forest, wetland, grassland, coastal, urban, backyard
- Weather, temperature, wind
- Identification: size class, plumage, beak shape, song / call
- Behavior: feeding, singing, flying, nesting, mating display, foraging
- Notes / field sketch space
- Lifer tag + photo / audio capture flags

**WHO THIS IS FOR**
- Beginning birders building their first life list

- Naturalists, hikers, and nature journalers

**WHY THIS JOURNAL**
Apps are great for fast counts, but paper teaches observation. Sketching a bird's posture, writing out a song in syllables, and noting habitat and behavior in your own hand sharpen the kind of attention that turns "sparrow" into "white-throated sparrow" — and lights up a lifetime hobby. This journal is light enough for a field bag, structured enough to pull observations out of even quick sightings, and roomy enough for the long entries that special days deserve.

**SPECS**

Title: Grief Journal for the Loss of a Parent
Description:
**The world is louder for everyone else now. Quieter for you.**

Losing a parent is the loss most people will live through and the loss most people are most alone in. The cards stop arriving. The casserole dishes go home. And then a Tuesday in February arrives and you cannot make it to the front door.

This guided journal sits with you on those Tuesdays. It is not a 12-step program. It is not a "moving on" playbook. It is a place - structured by someone who knows that grief is not a problem to be solved.

**INSIDE THIS JOURNAL**

**THEIR STORY** - memorial pages
- About my mother / father - name, voice, hands, laugh
- Who they were - what they loved, their faith, their hardest year
- Two photo placeholder pages with prompts ("when I look at this photo...")

**FIRST WEEK** - seven longer entries for the rawest days
- Day One - the first day without them
- Day Two - the people who showed up (and who didn't)
- Day Three - their empty place
- Day Four - the story of their last day
- Day Five - how my body is taking it
- Day Six - their voice in my head
- Day Seven - closing the first week

**60 DAILY CHECK-INS** - a 5-minute rhythm for the hardest two months
- Heaviness scale (1-10) - one word for today
- "I missed them most when..."
- Something that helped (or did not)
- One small thing for tomorrow

**WEEKLY REFLECTIONS** every ~10 days - to surface progress you cannot feel.

**MILESTONE PAGES** at 1 Month, 3 Months, 6 Months, and 1 Year - the longer reflections that honor where you are. Letters to them, letters to your Day-One self, the "firsts" you have carried.

**FIVE LETTER PAGES** - direct space to write to them. Dear ____________________.

**MEMORIAL LISTS**
- Ten Things They Taught Me
- Ten Memories I Never Want to Lose
- Their Sayings, Their Phrases
- Five Traits I Hope I Inherited

**THE STAGES OF GRIEF** - including David Kessler's sixth stage, *Meaning* - so you know nothing you feel is wrong.

**SUPPORT RESOURCES** - 988 Lifeline, SAMHSA, GriefShare, Modern Loss, The Dinner Party, and the books that have helped many readers (Megan Devine, Joan Didion, Hope Edelman).

**WHO THIS IS FOR**
- Adults grieving the recent death of a mother or father
- Adult children whose parent died years ago and the grief has resurfaced
- Sympathy gift for a friend, sibling, or coworker after a parent's passing
- Those navigating anticipatory grief during a parent's terminal illness

**FORMAT**
- 6 x 9 in - fits in a nightstand drawer or a handbag
- ~67 pages, soft cover, print-on-demand
- Photo placeholder boxes - tape or paste your own pictures inside
- Margins designed for left- and right-handed writers

**A NOTE ON LOVE**
The grief is the size of the love. It does not shrink. You learn to grow around it - and one day you find you have built a larger life because of who they were.

**You don't have to be ready. You only have to open the page.**

Title: Pet Loss Memorial Journal
Description:
**For the empty bowl. The silent house. The leash you can't put away yet.**

When you lose a pet, the world is louder for everyone else and quieter for you. This guided memorial journal sits beside you through the rawest days, then walks with you across the long arc of healing - while making a permanent, beautiful place for who they were.

This is not a "12-step" book. There is no schedule, no judgement, no platitudes. There is only space designed by someone who knows what 2am of pet grief feels like.

**INSIDE THIS JOURNAL**

**THEIR STORY** (memorial pages, fillable)
- About my best friend - name, breed, markings, the look in their eyes
- How we found each other
- Who they were - quirks, sounds, things they loved and hated
- Photo memorial pages with prompts ("their best day", "just their eyes")

**FIRST WEEK PAGES** (7 longer entries)
- The first day without them
- Their empty space at home
- How my body is taking it
- The story of our last day
- Who has shown up - and who hasn't
- Their voice in my head
- Closing the first week

**60 DAILY GRIEF CHECK-INS** (3-5 minutes each)
- Heaviness scale (1-10)
- One word for today
- "I missed them most when..."
- Something that helped today
- A memory I want to keep
- One small thing for tomorrow

**WEEKLY REFLECTIONS** every 10 days to surface progress you can't feel.

**MILESTONE PAGES** at One Month, Three Months, Six Months, and One Year - the longer reflections that honor where you are.

**LETTER PAGES** - direct space to write to them. As many times as you need to.

**MEMORIAL LISTS** - "Ten memories I never want to lose" + "Their daily rituals" - protect the details from time.

**THE STAGES OF PET GRIEF** - a brief, compassionate explanation of denial, guilt, anger, sadness, and meaning - so you know none of what you're feeling is wrong.

**SUPPORT RESOURCES** - ASPCA Pet Loss Hotline, Cornell Pet Loss Line, Lap of Love, 988 Crisis Line, and trusted books on pet grief.

**WHO THIS IS FOR**
- Anyone who recently lost a dog, cat, rabbit, or other companion animal
- Children and teens grieving a family pet (the prompts are gentle enough)
- Sympathy gift for a friend or family member after a pet's passing
- Those still grieving years later who need a place to put it

**FORMAT**
- 6 x 9 inches - fits in a nightstand drawer
- ~95 pages, soft cover, print-on-demand
- Photo placeholder boxes - tape or paste your own pictures inside

**A NOTE ON LOVE**
The grief is the size of the love. You are not "too sensitive" or "too attached." You are a human who loved an animal completely - and that is the highest thing a heart can do.

**You don't have to be ready. You only have to open the page.**
